findings.title: Bot/noisy-rules queries scoped to 30 days (was unbounded)
findings.description: The FINDINGS_BY_BOT_QUERY adds AND created_at >= TIMESTAMP_SUB(...) which changes semantics: previously the query returned ALL findings for a bot (no time bound), now it is capped at 30 days. Same change applies to NOISY_RULES_QUERY. This is a correct bug fix for partition filtering, but may surprise dashboard users expecting historical bot analysis.

findings.title: Schema fields added without population code in this PR
findings.description: hasFindingsMin and latestReviewId are documented as "Denormalised by completeReview / recordFinding" but neither function is modified in this PR. The fields will remain null until a follow-up change populates them. If intentional schema-first work, a tracking issue should be referenced.

findings.title: hasFindingsMin enum values mismatch actual severity scale
findings.description: The new hasFindingsMin enum declares ["critical", "high", "medium", "low", "info"], but publishFinding passes finding.severity straight through — and callers emit BLOCKER / MAJOR / MINOR / NIT. The denormalised field can never match any of the declared enum values, so it will always be null.

findings.title: Finding/review publish bypass schema validation, PR does not
findings.description: publishFinding and publishReviewCompleted build raw payloads and call Pub/Sub publish directly, while publishPrEvent goes through validateAndPublish which validates the domain object against the JSON schema. This means findings and reviews can publish malformed data without any schema guard.

findings.title: extraFields may inject old field names into new BQ payload
findings.description: The diff renames many top-level payload fields to match the BQ schema. However, if any callers of publishPrOpened/publishPrSynchronized/publishPrMerged/publishPrClosed pass extraFields containing old field names (e.g. pr_created_at, repo_full_name), those old fields will appear in the payload alongside the new ones. REQUIRED field renames (e.g. pr_created_at → created_at) mean the old name is now missing — any consumer expecting backward-compatible field names will break.

findings.title: Out-of-scope comment references the issue this PR fixes
findings.description: The comment says "Reconciliation is tracked out-of-scope to #1789" but #1789 is the issue this PR closes. That makes the tracking reference circular: the debt would be "tracked" in the very issue being resolved. It should reference a separate follow-up issue number.

findings.title: Pub/Sub attr renamed repo_full_name to repository_full_name
findings.description: repo_full_name was renamed to repository_full_name in the PR event Pub/Sub attributes. Any downstream subscription filter or consumer that matches on repo_full_name will silently stop receiving these messages. No comment or migration note documents the breaking change.

findings.title: PR funnel query breaks on pre-migration BQ rows
findings.description: The PR funnel query changes from first_review_at IS NOT NULL to stats.time_to_first_review_ms IS NOT NULL. Old PR documents in BigQuery written before this publisher change may have first_review_at but NOT the nested stats.time_to_first_review_ms, causing those PRs to appear as 'no first review' and distorting funnel metrics.

findings.title: pr.id could be null for REQUIRED BQ column
findings.description: The payload now includes id: pr.id as a REQUIRED column. If pr.id is undefined or null for any edge-case PR object, the message will be dead-lettered silently by BigQuery. A runtime assertion or fallback would prevent this.

findings.title: Timestamp fields passed raw without ISO normalization
findings.description: Fields like pr.openedAt, pr.mergedAt, pr.closedAt are nullable but passed through without normalization. If any value is non-null but not in ISO 8601 format, BigQuery will reject the row. This risk is amplified by the schema becoming stricter with use_table_schema = true + drop_unknown_fields = true.
